name: Lithuania
avoid: November–February for short daylight, icy pavements, and a cold Baltic coast
Peak summer weekends in Nida if you dislike traffic and premium hotel rates

cities.name: Vilnius
cities.notes: Baroque Old Town with Gediminas Castle Tower, Užupis lanes and strong café culture

cities.name: Kaunas
cities.notes: Interwar modernist city with a castle, funiculars and Lithuania's best museum cluster

cities.name: Trakai
cities.notes: Lake-ringed former capital known for Trakai Island Castle and Karaim kibinai

cities.name: Klaipėda
cities.notes: Baltic port with a compact German-influenced old town and easy ferry links to the dunes

cities.name: Curonian Spit
cities.notes: UNESCO-protected sand-dune peninsula centred on Nida and Juodkrantė

itinerary.sim: Pildyk or Ežys prepaid SIMs are the easiest tourist pick in Lithuania, usually about ₹450–₹900 for 20–60GB; buy them at Vilnius Airport kiosks, Maxima supermarkets, or telecom shops in the city centres.

Vilnius.name: Vilnius
Vilnius.note: Three days is ideal for Vilnius: one classic Old Town day, one museum-and-Užupis day, and one church-and-viewpoint day without rushing the food scene.

days.theme: Old Town, Cathedral Square & Gediminas Hill

activities.name: Cathedral Square, Pilies Street, and the Old Town lanes
Gediminas Castle Tower
activities.tip: Go early or late for the best light over the red roofs and fewer tour groups.

activities.name: Palace of the Grand Dukes main route
St. Anne's Church and Bernardine quarter walk
Museum of Occupations and Freedom Fights
MO Museum
Užupis republic walk and riverfront art stops
Vilnius Cathedral Bell Tower

days.theme: University Courtyards, Baroque Churches & Market Grazing

activities.name: Vilnius University courtyards and St. John's Bell Tower
Church of Sts. Peter and Paul
Three Crosses Hill viewpoint
Halės Market snack crawl

Trakai.name: Trakai
Trakai.note: Trakai works best as a relaxed overnight or full-day stop between Vilnius and Kaunas; one day is enough for the castle, lakefront, and Karaim food.

days.theme: Island Castle, Karaim Heritage & the Lakeshore

activities.name: Trakai Island Castle museum
activities.tip: The first hour after opening is much calmer than late-morning coach-tour time in summer.

activities.name: Karaim Street wooden houses and kenesa exterior walk
Church of the Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Galvė Lake boat ride or pedal boat rental

Kaunas.name: Kaunas
Kaunas.note: Two days lets you cover Kaunas Old Town properly and still give modernist museums and memorial history the time they deserve.

days.theme: Old Town, Castle Precinct & Laisvės Alėja

activities.name: Kaunas Old Town and Town Hall Square walk
Kaunas Castle Museum
Kaunas City Museum at Town Hall
Laisvės alėja boulevard and St. Michael the Archangel Church
Ninth Fort Museum
Devils' Museum
M. K. Čiurlionis National Museum of Art
Žaliakalnis Funicular and Christ's Resurrection Church rooftop

Klaipėda.name: Klaipėda
Klaipėda.note: A full day covers Klaipėda's compact old town well; add a second only if you want more museum time before crossing to the Curonian Spit.

days.theme: Old Town, Castle Area & the Dane Riverfront

activities.name: Theatre Square, Meridianas, and the Old Town lanes
Castle Museum
Ferry to Smiltynė and harbourfront walk
Lithuanian Sea Museum

Curonian Spit.name: Curonian Spit
Curonian Spit.note: Even one full day on the spit gives you dunes, Nida, and a museum stop, but staying overnight is far more rewarding once the day-trippers leave.

days.theme: Nida Dunes, Quiet Harbours & Amber Museums

activities.name: Parnidis Dune and sundial viewpoint
activities.tip: Sunrise and the final hour before sunset are the best times for the dune ridge and lagoon views.

activities.name: Thomas Mann Museum
Mizgiris Amber Museum
Nida harbour walk and beach time

connections.method: LTG Link train from Vilnius station to Trakai (about 30–35m)
Return to Vilnius and continue by LTG Link train to Kaunas (about 1h 45m–2h 10m total rail time with the transfer)
LTG Link train from Kaunas to Klaipėda (about 3h 45m–4h 15m)
Pedestrian ferry to Smiltynė plus the local Neringa bus onward to Nida (about 2h–2h 20m total)
Local bus and ferry back to Klaipėda, then a Lux Express coach to Vilnius (about 5h 30m–6h 30m total)

extras: Lithuania's national museums are often free on the last Sunday of the month, so time Vilnius or Kaunas museum-heavy days around that if you can.
LTG Link is the easiest way to do Vilnius, Trakai, and Kaunas without a car, while Lux Express is the most comfortable long-haul option back from Klaipėda.
For the Curonian Spit, overnight in Nida if possible: the dunes and harbour feel much quieter after the last ferry crowds return to Klaipėda.
Kibinai in Trakai and smoked fish on the Curonian Spit are the two easiest regional food wins to prioritise on a short trip.
